decomposition_notes: Split along the three orthogonal seams the requirements doc explicitly calls out: (G1) server-side contract changes, (G2) the five remaining plugin-variant producer changes (G142/W684-W687 already cover the `stride` variant — NOT duplicated here), (G3) consumer-side Kanban UI changes that build on G1's validator. CLAIM ORDER: G1 (contract) MUST land in main first because both G2 (producer payloads need the validator to accept them) and G3 (UI reads structured fields the validator gates) depend on it. G2 and G3 can proceed in parallel after G1. G2's first task (locking the canonical schema in the `stride` repo docs) depends on G142's W684 having defined the base schema — express that as an identifier dependency ["W684"] on the schema-doc task. G3's panel-component task is independent of G2 because the validator (G1) is the only true blocker for the consumer surface. Schema location decision (open question in the doc): canonical schema lives in the `stride` repo (since G142 already placed it in stride/agents/task-reviewer.md), referenced by the other five variants — encoded into G2's first task. Acceptance-grid enum decision (open question): use `met` / `not_met` (underscore form) to match G142's parent schema and avoid spaces in JSON enum values — encoded into G1's validator task. The reviewer flagged this scope as potentially too large for one goal; three sibling goals at 6–8 tasks each fits the ~10-task soft cap cleanly and matches the producer/contract/consumer feature areas.

goals.title: Extend Kanban server-side contract for structured reviewer_result payloads
goals.why: Today's CompletionValidation accepts only summary + integer counts in reviewer_result. To unlock structured emission from the six plugin variants and structured rendering in the UI, the server must accept and persist the richer payload (issues[], acceptance_criteria[], testing_strategy, patterns, pitfalls, schema_version) while remaining backwards compatible with the legacy shape.
goals.what: Extend Kanban.Tasks.CompletionValidation to validate the new optional structured fields, ensure Task serialization passes them through verbatim, update agent_json.ex reviewer_result_format documentation, and document the canonical schema reference. No DB migration needed (reviewer_result is already :jsonb).
goals.description: Server-side contract layer of the end-to-end structured review-report rollout. Must land before plugin producers (G2) and Kanban consumers (G3) can rely on the new shape. Backwards compatibility is non-negotiable: the legacy summary-only payload must continue to validate.
goals.acceptance_criteria:
validate_reviewer_result/1 accepts the new optional fields (issues, acceptance_criteria, testing_strategy, patterns, pitfalls, schema_version) and validates shape + enum values
Legacy summary-only payloads continue to validate without changes
Unknown forward-compatible fields are tolerated (not rejected)
Task serialization passes new fields through verbatim to API consumers
agent_json.ex reviewer_result_format description documents the new schema
Unit tests cover happy path, legacy compatibility path, and rejection of malformed shapes
pitfalls: Do not reject payloads that omit new fields — legacy clients must keep working
Do not reject payloads containing unknown fields — forward compatibility is a stated success metric
Do not change the existing review_report markdown emission path; that's a parallel field

tasks.title: Extend CompletionValidation to accept structured issues[] and acceptance_criteria[] arrays
tasks.description: Add validation clauses in Kanban.Tasks.CompletionValidation for the new optional `issues` and `acceptance_criteria` array fields on reviewer_result. Each issue must validate severity (critical/high/medium/low), category (string), and optional file/line/description/suggested_fix fields. Each acceptance_criterion must validate id, status (met/not_met enum), and optional notes.
tasks.why: Without server-side acceptance of these arrays, plugin producers cannot PATCH the richer payload and the UI has nothing structured to render.
tasks.what: Add private validation helpers for issues array (with severity + category enums) and acceptance_criteria array (with met/not_met status enum). Compose them into validate_reviewer_result/1 as optional fields. Reject malformed entries with descriptive errors; tolerate omission entirely.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
validate_reviewer_result/1 accepts payloads containing well-formed issues[] and acceptance_criteria[] arrays
Returns {:error, descriptive_message} for malformed issue entries (wrong severity enum, missing required fields)
Returns {:error, descriptive_message} for malformed acceptance_criterion entries (status not in met/not_met)
Legacy payloads (no issues, no acceptance_criteria) continue to validate successfully
Acceptance criterion status uses underscore form `not_met` (matches G142 parent schema, avoids spaces in JSON enums)
Unit tests cover: well-formed structured payload, malformed severity, malformed status, legacy summary-only payload, missing arrays entirely
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Mirror existing validation helper structure in completion_validation.ex
Use {:ok, payload} | {:error, message} return shape consistent with the existing module
Use pattern matching in function heads for enum validation rather than nested case statements
pitfalls: Do not require either array — both are optional
Do not use `not met` with a space; use `not_met` to match the canonical schema
Do not raise — return {:error, _} tuples

key_files.note: Add validators for issues[] and acceptance_criteria[]
Unit tests for new validators and legacy compatibility

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass including legacy-shape compatibility tests

verification_steps.step_text: mix credo --strict lib/kanban/tasks/completion_validation.ex
verification_steps.expected_result: No warnings

unit_tests: Well-formed structured payload accepted
Malformed severity enum rejected with descriptive error
Malformed acceptance-criterion status rejected
Legacy summary-only payload still validates
Empty/missing arrays tolerated
edge_cases: Mixed legacy + structured fields in same payload
Nested unknown fields inside an issue entry

tasks.title: Extend CompletionValidation to accept testing_strategy, patterns, pitfalls, schema_version fields
tasks.description: Add validation for the three section-verdict fields (testing_strategy, patterns, pitfalls) and the schema_version semver string. Each section verdict is an object with a status field and optional notes; schema_version is a string the validator accepts when present but does not require.
tasks.why: These fields are first-class in the new schema and replace what the kanban app currently regex-extracts from the markdown report.
tasks.what: Add private helpers validating each section verdict's shape (status enum + optional notes) and a semver-shaped schema_version string. Wire into validate_reviewer_result/1 as optional fields, tolerating absence entirely.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
validate_reviewer_result/1 accepts payloads containing well-formed testing_strategy, patterns, pitfalls section-verdict objects
schema_version is accepted when present (semver-shaped string) and tolerated when absent
Malformed section-verdict status returns {:error, descriptive_message}
Legacy payloads (no section verdicts, no schema_version) continue to validate
Unit tests cover each new field's happy + error paths and legacy omission
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Reuse the section-verdict shape across all three fields (testing_strategy, patterns, pitfalls) via a shared helper
Keep schema_version validation permissive — accept any semver-shaped string; do not gate on specific version values
pitfalls: Do not require schema_version even though the doc says emitters should write "1.0" — the contract is backwards compatible
Do not couple this task's validators to a specific schema version number

key_files.note: Add validators for section verdicts and schema_version

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass

verification_steps.step_text: mix credo --strict lib/kanban/tasks/completion_validation.ex
verification_steps.expected_result: No warnings

Malformed status in any section verdict rejected
schema_version present and well-formed accepted
schema_version absent accepted (backwards compat)
All new fields absent (legacy payload) accepted

All structured fields together in one payload

tasks.title: Ensure Task and API JSON serialization pass structured reviewer_result through verbatim
tasks.description: Confirm Kanban.Tasks.Task's reviewer_result jsonb field round-trips arbitrary nested structures without loss, and that lib/kanban_web/controllers/api/task_json.ex serializes reviewer_result verbatim in API responses. Add explicit tests asserting structured payloads survive insert → reload → render.
tasks.why: The success metric requires that future schema additions persist with no Kanban deploy. That only holds if persistence and serialization are truly verbatim.
tasks.what: Audit the Task schema's reviewer_result field declaration (already :map per requirements doc) and the task_json.ex render functions to confirm no field filtering occurs. Add tests asserting a payload with all new structured fields persists and serializes unchanged. Update completion_result_gate.ex if it currently strips unknown fields.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
A reviewer_result payload containing all new structured fields persists to the database and reloads identically
API JSON output for a task with structured reviewer_result includes all fields verbatim
completion_result_gate.ex does not strip unknown fields (or is updated to stop doing so)
Unknown forward-compatible fields survive the round trip
Tests assert byte-equivalent round trip for a representative payload
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
If Task uses :map for reviewer_result, no schema change is needed — only tests
If task_json.ex currently filters fields, replace the filter with a pass-through Map.get(task, :reviewer_result)
pitfalls: Do not introduce a new Ecto type for reviewer_result — :map jsonb is the documented shape
Do not add field allowlisting in task_json.ex — that would defeat forward compatibility

goals.title: Roll out structured reviewer payload to five remaining plugin variants
goals.why: G142 (W684–W687) covers the structured-emission contract for the `stride` plugin only. The other five variants (stride-copilot, stride-codex, stride-gemini, stride-opencode, stride-pi) emit the same review payload and must be updated in lockstep — otherwise the fleet stays permanently fragmented and the 14-day all-variants leading indicator fails.
goals.what: For each of the five remaining plugin variants: update task-reviewer prompt to emit the expanded schema, update workflow/completing-tasks skill to extract the new fields into the PATCH payload, bump CHANGELOG, tag, push, bump marketplace entry, push marketplace tag. The canonical schema lives in the `stride` repo (per G142's W684) and is referenced from each variant.
goals.where_context: Five external git repos: stride-copilot, stride-codex, stride-gemini, stride-opencode, stride-pi (each with their own task-reviewer agent prompt + workflow skill + CHANGELOG + marketplace entry)
goals.description: Producer-side rollout for the five non-`stride` plugin variants. Depends on G1 (the Kanban contract must accept the new payloads before producers emit them) and on G142's W684 (the canonical schema document must exist in the stride repo to reference). Release order is documented per-task: stride-copilot first (highest-traffic), then stride-codex, stride-gemini, stride-opencode, stride-pi alphabetically.
goals.acceptance_criteria:
All five non-stride plugin variants emit the structured reviewer payload matching the canonical schema
All five variants' workflow/completing-tasks skill extracts the new fields into the PATCH payload
All five variants' CHANGELOG, plugin tag, marketplace entry, and marketplace tag are bumped
Leading indicator achievable: all six plugin variants reflect the new contract in marketplace JSON within 14 days
pitfalls: Do not redefine the schema per-variant — reference the canonical schema in the stride repo
Do not split the release across variants on different days if avoidable — fragmentation is the failure mode
Do not skip the marketplace bump — without it, users running plugin install pick up the old version

tasks.title: Lock canonical reviewer_result schema document in stride repo for cross-variant reference
tasks.description: Finalize the canonical JSON schema document in the stride repo (location established by G142's W684) so the five remaining variant prompts can reference it by URL/path. Confirm the document includes schema_version, all section verdicts, and the met/not_met enum form. Add a short README note in the stride repo declaring this file the schema of record.
tasks.why: Open question in the requirements doc: schema location of record. Decision: stride repo, since G142 already places the schema there. This task makes that decision authoritative before the five variant updates begin.
tasks.what: Audit the schema document W684 produced in stride/agents/task-reviewer.md (or wherever W684 placed it) for completeness against the contract validated in G1. Add a top-of-file comment naming this the canonical schema. Reference path/URL ready to be cited from the other five variants.
tasks.where_context: External stride repo (separate from the kanban codebase): typically stride/agents/task-reviewer.md and stride/README.md
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Schema document in stride repo names itself as canonical (top-of-file comment or README note)
Schema covers schema_version, summary, status, issue_counts, issues[], acceptance_criteria[] (with met/not_met enum), testing_strategy, patterns, pitfalls
Reference path is documented somewhere the variant prompts can cite
No functional code change to the stride repo beyond documentation
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Mirror the documentation tone of G142's W684 output
Use the same met/not_met enum form as the Kanban validator (G1 task 0)
pitfalls: Do not duplicate the schema in a third location — single source of truth
Do not change the schema shape G142 already shipped — only annotate canonicality

tasks.title: Release stride-copilot plugin and marketplace bump

tasks.why: Until the marketplace entry bumps and the plugin tag exists on origin, users cannot pick up the new version. Both bumps are required to satisfy the 14-day leading indicator.
tasks.what: Follow the documented release procedure mirroring G142's W687 (stride/CHANGELOG bump + tag + marketplace bump): edit CHANGELOG.md in stride-copilot, commit, tag, push origin + tags; then edit stride-marketplace plugin entry, commit, tag, push origin + tags.

goals.title: Build shared ReviewReportPanel and wire into review, show, and edit surfaces
goals.why: Today's /review screen extracts review data by regex-parsing the markdown review_report field; Task Show and Task Edit surface even less. To meet the Outcome (structured rendering across all three surfaces, regex parsing demoted to legacy fallback) the consumer side needs a single reused panel component that detects-and-degrades and a telemetry counter so the leading indicator (`review.fallback_used` stays at 0 for new tasks) is observable.
goals.what: Build KanbanWeb.ReviewReportPanel as a reusable LiveComponent that renders structured payloads when reviewer_result.issues is present and falls through to the existing markdown regex path otherwise. Wire it into ReviewLive, Task Show, and Task Edit. Add review.fallback_used and review.structured_used telemetry counters. Update ReviewStatsStrip to source cells from structured fields when available. Retain legacy regex helpers as the fallback path.
goals.where_context: lib/kanban_web/components/ (new ReviewReportPanel), lib/kanban_web/live/review_live.ex (replace ad-hoc rendering, retain helpers as fallback), Task Show and Task Edit LiveViews, lib/kanban_web/components/review_*.ex (existing review components for reference)
goals.description: Consumer-side rollout. Depends on G1 (the validator must accept structured payloads before the UI can rely on them). Does NOT depend on G2 because the panel's detect-and-degrade behavior means it works whether or not producers are emitting structured data yet — structured rendering activates per-task as G2's plugin releases land. The legacy regex path remains permanent for pre-rollout tasks.
goals.acceptance_criteria:
KanbanWeb.ReviewReportPanel LiveComponent renders structured payload when reviewer_result.issues is a list
Panel falls through to existing markdown regex path when reviewer_result.issues is absent and review_report is present
Panel renders nothing when both are absent
Panel is wired into /review queue, Task Show, and Task Edit
ReviewStatsStrip sources cells from structured fields when available
review.fallback_used and review.structured_used telemetry counters fire once per render
Legacy regex helpers in review_live.ex retained as fallback code path
Skip_form tasks continue to render via existing summary-and-skip-reason layout (no structured grid, no issue list)
Dark mode verified on the new panel per CLAUDE.md guidelines
pitfalls: Do not delete the legacy regex helpers — they are the permanent fallback for pre-rollout tasks
Do not call Ecto.Repo from the panel component — go through the appropriate context module
Do not skip dark-mode verification on the new panel per repository CLAUDE.md mandate
Do not change the skip_form rendering path — it is explicitly out of scope per the requirements doc Non-goals

tasks.title: Add review.fallback_used and review.structured_used telemetry counter events
tasks.description: Define two telemetry events the panel will emit per render: [:kanban, :review, :fallback_used] and [:kanban, :review, :structured_used]. Wire a handler that increments counters so the leading indicator (fallback_used stays at 0 for new tasks) is observable.
tasks.why: Success metric explicitly cites `review.fallback_used` and `review.structured_used` counters as the leading-indicator measurement mechanism. Without them, the leading indicator is unmeasurable.
tasks.what: Define the two telemetry event names in a central telemetry module, wire counter handlers in Kanban.Telemetry (or wherever existing telemetry events live), and document the metric names so the panel can emit them in later tasks.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Two telemetry events [:kanban, :review, :fallback_used] and [:kanban, :review, :structured_used] are defined and documented
Counter handlers are wired and observable via the existing telemetry surface
Unit test verifies the events fire when invoked
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Follow the existing telemetry event registration pattern in the kanban app
Use atom-list event names consistent with other [:kanban, ...] events
pitfalls: Do not emit the events yet from the panel — that happens in the panel-build task
Do not introduce a new telemetry library — reuse what's already wired

key_files.note: Register new telemetry events and handlers

verification_steps.step_text: mix test test/kanban/telemetry_test.exs
verification_steps.expected_result: Telemetry tests pass including new event registration

unit_tests: Telemetry events fire when invoked
Counter handlers increment correctly

tasks.title: Build KanbanWeb.ReviewReportPanel LiveComponent with detect-and-degrade rendering
tasks.description: Create a new LiveComponent at lib/kanban_web/components/review_report_panel.ex that takes a task and renders: (a) structured view when reviewer_result['issues'] is a list — showing status, summary, severity-grouped issue list, acceptance grid, and three section verdicts; (b) markdown-fallback view by delegating to the existing review_live.ex regex helpers when issues is absent and review_report is present; (c) nothing when both are absent. Emit the appropriate telemetry counter on each render.
tasks.why: Single reused panel across three surfaces is the Outcome's core consumer change. Detect-and-degrade keeps pre-rollout tasks rendering via the legacy path forever.
tasks.what: New LiveComponent with three render branches gated on reviewer_result shape. Structured branch builds a severity-grouped issue list, an acceptance grid with met/not_met badges, and three section-verdict tiles. Fallback branch calls into existing review_live.ex helpers (testing_strategy_value, patterns_value, pitfalls_value, report_section). Emit telemetry on each render path.
tasks.where_context: lib/kanban_web/components/review_report_panel.ex (new), lib/kanban_web/live/review_live.ex (reference existing helpers for fallback delegation)
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
ReviewReportPanel LiveComponent exists at lib/kanban_web/components/review_report_panel.ex
Structured branch renders when reviewer_result['issues'] is a list
Fallback branch renders when issues absent and review_report present, delegating to existing regex helpers
Empty branch renders nothing when both absent
review.structured_used telemetry fires on structured render
review.fallback_used telemetry fires on fallback render
Dark mode verified per CLAUDE.md guidelines (text-base-content, bg-base-100, border-base-300)
Component uses core_components.ex primitives where applicable
Unit tests cover all three render branches
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Use existing core_components.ex primitives (<.icon>, <.button>, etc.) instead of inventing custom styles
Use daisyUI semantic tokens (bg-base-100, text-base-content, border-base-300) per CLAUDE.md dark-mode guidelines
Delegate fallback rendering to existing review_live.ex helpers — do NOT duplicate the regex logic
Met/not_met enum values rendered with consistent badge styling
pitfalls: Do not call Kanban.Repo from the component — accept the task as an assign
Do not duplicate the regex helpers — delegate to review_live.ex
Do not hardcode colors like text-gray-900 or bg-white — use daisyUI tokens per CLAUDE.md
Do not skip the empty branch — both fields can legitimately be absent

key_files.note: Reference existing regex helpers for fallback delegation

verification_steps.step_text: mix test test/kanban_web/components/review_report_panel_test.exs
verification_steps.expected_result: All render-branch tests pass

verification_steps.step_text: Render a structured-payload task in both light and dark mode per CLAUDE.md verification process
verification_steps.expected_result: Component renders correctly in both modes with proper contrast

unit_tests: Structured branch renders with all sections
Fallback branch delegates to legacy helpers
Empty branch renders nothing
Telemetry events fire on appropriate paths
edge_cases: Acceptance criteria array present but empty

tasks.title: Wire ReviewReportPanel into ReviewLive replacing ad-hoc rendering
tasks.description: Replace the current ad-hoc review-data rendering in lib/kanban_web/live/review_live.ex (which directly invokes the regex helpers in the template) with a single call to the new ReviewReportPanel LiveComponent. Retain the helper functions themselves — they are now the fallback path the panel delegates to.
tasks.why: /review queue is the highest-traffic consumer of review data. Wiring it through the panel is what flips the production code path from regex-extraction to structured rendering for new tasks.
tasks.what: Edit the ReviewLive template to replace ad-hoc rendering calls with <.live_component module={ReviewReportPanel} task={@task} id={...} />. Leave the helper module functions in place — they are called by the panel's fallback branch.
tasks.where_context: lib/kanban_web/live/review_live.ex and its HEEx template (if separated)
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
ReviewLive template invokes ReviewReportPanel instead of ad-hoc rendering
Legacy regex helpers (testing_strategy_value, patterns_value, pitfalls_value, report_section) remain in review_live.ex for the panel's fallback branch to call
For a task with structured reviewer_result, /review renders the structured view
For a task with only markdown review_report, /review renders the fallback view
Existing ReviewLive tests pass (updated where the panel now owns rendering)
Dark mode verified on /review per CLAUDE.md guidelines
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Follow the existing live_component invocation pattern used elsewhere in the kanban app
Keep ReviewLive's mount/handle_event logic intact — only rendering changes
pitfalls: Do not delete the regex helpers — they are now the fallback path
Do not change ReviewLive's data-loading logic — only the template
Do not break skip_form rendering — verify a skip_form task still renders correctly

key_files.note: Replace ad-hoc rendering with panel invocation; retain helpers
Update tests to reflect panel-driven rendering

verification_steps.step_text: mix test test/kanban_web/live/review_live_test.exs
verification_steps.expected_result: All ReviewLive tests pass

verification_steps.step_text: Visit /review with a structured-payload task in both light and dark mode
verification_steps.expected_result: Structured view renders correctly in both modes

verification_steps.step_text: Visit /review with a legacy markdown-only task
verification_steps.expected_result: Fallback view renders correctly

unit_tests: ReviewLive renders panel for structured task
ReviewLive renders panel for legacy markdown task
ReviewLive renders nothing in panel when both fields absent

tasks.title: Wire ReviewReportPanel into Task Show LiveView
tasks.description: Add a ReviewReportPanel section to the Task Show LiveView so that opening any task with review data displays the same panel users see in /review. The panel's detect-and-degrade behavior handles all three states (structured / fallback / empty).
tasks.why: Outcome explicitly names Task Show as one of three surfaces that must render the structured payload. Today Task Show surfaces less than /review does.
tasks.what: Edit the Task Show LiveView template to include <.live_component module={ReviewReportPanel} task={@task} id={...} /> in an appropriate section (typically below task metadata, above comments).
tasks.where_context: Task Show LiveView under lib/kanban_web/live/ (exact filename varies — locate the show view for tasks)
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Task Show template invokes ReviewReportPanel
For a task with structured reviewer_result, Task Show renders the structured view
For a task with only markdown review_report, Task Show renders the fallback view
For a task with no review data, the panel section is absent or empty
Existing Task Show tests pass
Dark mode verified per CLAUDE.md guidelines
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Match the placement and styling pattern used by ReviewLive's panel invocation
Keep Task Show's existing mount/handle_event logic intact
pitfalls: Do not load review data separately — the task assign already carries reviewer_result and review_report
Do not skip dark-mode verification

key_files.note: Add panel invocation in template (path may differ)

verification_steps.step_text: mix test test/kanban_web/live/task_live/
verification_steps.expected_result: All Task LiveView tests pass

verification_steps.step_text: Visit Task Show for a task with structured reviewer_result in both light and dark mode
verification_steps.expected_result: Panel renders correctly in both modes

unit_tests: Task Show renders panel for tasks with review data
Task Show omits panel section for tasks with no review data
edge_cases: Task with no review data at all

tasks.title: Wire ReviewReportPanel into Task Edit LiveView
tasks.description: Add a ReviewReportPanel section to the Task Edit LiveView mirroring the Task Show wiring. The panel is read-only in this iteration (editing reviewer_result is out of scope).
tasks.why: Outcome explicitly names Task Edit as the third surface that must render the structured payload.
tasks.what: Edit the Task Edit LiveView template to include <.live_component module={ReviewReportPanel} task={@task} id={...} /> in an appropriate section. Confirm no edit affordances are introduced on the panel — it is read-only.
tasks.where_context: Task Edit LiveView under lib/kanban_web/live/ (exact filename varies — locate the edit view for tasks)
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Task Edit template invokes ReviewReportPanel
Panel renders read-only (no edit affordances on review data)
Existing Task Edit tests pass
Dark mode verified per CLAUDE.md guidelines
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Mirror the Task Show wiring exactly
Keep Task Edit's form-handling logic untouched
pitfalls: Do not add edit affordances on the panel — editing reviewer_result is out of scope
Do not skip dark-mode verification

key_files.note: Add panel invocation in template (path may differ)

verification_steps.step_text: mix test test/kanban_web/live/task_live/
verification_steps.expected_result: All Task LiveView tests pass

verification_steps.step_text: Visit Task Edit for a task with structured reviewer_result in both light and dark mode
verification_steps.expected_result: Panel renders read-only in both modes

unit_tests: Task Edit renders panel read-only
manual_tests: Confirm no edit affordances on review data

tasks.title: Update ReviewStatsStrip to source cells from structured fields when available
tasks.description: Update KanbanWeb.ReviewStatsStrip (or equivalent existing review-stats component) so that when reviewer_result carries structured fields, the stats cells pull from those fields (e.g., issue counts by severity from the issues array) rather than from regex extraction. Fall back to existing behavior when structured fields are absent.
tasks.why: Today the stats strip pulls from the markdown report via regex. Once producers emit structured data, the stats strip should use the higher-fidelity source.
tasks.what: Edit the existing review-stats component to check for reviewer_result['issues'] (and other structured fields) first; if present, compute cells from the structured source; otherwise call existing computation. Add a test exercising both paths.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
ReviewStatsStrip prefers structured fields when reviewer_result carries them
Falls back to existing regex-based computation when structured fields absent
Existing stats-strip tests pass; new tests cover both source paths
Dark mode verified per CLAUDE.md guidelines
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Encapsulate the source-selection logic in a single helper so the component template stays simple
Do not duplicate the regex helpers — reuse review_live.ex helpers for the fallback path
pitfalls: Do not change the cells the stats strip displays — only the data source
Do not break the fallback path for legacy tasks

key_files.note: Update data sourcing to prefer structured fields (filename may differ slightly)

verification_steps.step_text: mix test test/kanban_web/components/
verification_steps.expected_result: All component tests pass including ReviewStatsStrip

verification_steps.step_text: Compare stats-strip output for a structured-payload task vs a legacy markdown task in both light and dark mode
verification_steps.expected_result: Both render correct counts; structured path matches structured data, fallback path matches regex extraction

unit_tests: Structured path computes cells from issues array
Fallback path computes cells from regex extraction
Cell display unchanged across both paths

tasks.title: Run full Kanban precommit suite after consumer changes
tasks.description: Run mix precommit, mix test --cover, and mix credo --strict to confirm the consumer-side changes pass all repository quality gates. Address any regressions before G3 ships.
tasks.why: Per repository CLAUDE.md quality guidelines, every completed task must pass precommit, coverage, and credo. Without this gate the goal cannot be considered done.
tasks.what: Run mix precommit, mix test --cover, mix credo --strict in sequence. Fix any failures introduced by tasks 0-5 of this goal.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
mix precommit passes
mix test --cover passes and coverage stays at or above the existing threshold
mix credo --strict passes with no new warnings
Dark mode verified end-to-end on all three surfaces (/review, Task Show, Task Edit) per CLAUDE.md
Gettext translations extracted and merged for any new UI text per repository convention
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Use the project's documented precommit alias
Run mix gettext.extract --merge if any new UI text was added in tasks 1-5
Do not skip credo or coverage gates
pitfalls: Do not reduce coverage thresholds to make tests pass
Do not skip gettext extract/merge — repository convention is to translate all visible UI text

verification_steps.step_text: mix precommit
verification_steps.expected_result: Precommit suite passes

verification_steps.step_text: mix test --cover
verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass; coverage at or above threshold

verification_steps.step_text: mix credo --strict
verification_steps.expected_result: No warnings

verification_steps.step_text: mix gettext.extract --merge
verification_steps.expected_result: Translations extracted and merged for any new UI text across all locales

manual_tests: End-to-end dark mode verification on all three surfaces
testing_strategy.coverage_target: At or above existing repository threshold
